title: cordia subcordata
intro: Cordia subcordata, also known as the New Zealand native tree or harakeke, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce the risk of infection. The health benefits of Cordia subcordata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Cordia subcordata is often used in tea or tincture form to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
